zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan De Normanville於1225年出生在英國英格蘭盧特藍德艾平加姆時，她的父親John de Normanville26歲，母親Agnes of York42歲。她和Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。她去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan De Normanville于1225年出生在英国英格兰盧特藍德艾平加姆时，她的父亲John de Normanville26岁，母亲Agnes of York42岁。她和Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。她去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan De Normanville was born in 1225, in Empingham, Rutland, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Normanville, was 26 and her mother, Agnes of York, was 42. She had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. She died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan De Normanville est née en 1225, à Empingham, Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Normanville, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Agnes of York, avait 42 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. Elle est décédée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan De Normanville 1225 in Empingham, Rutland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Normanville 26 und ihre Mutter Agnes of York 42. Sie hatte mit Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De Normanville è nata il nel 1225 nel Empingham, Rutland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Normanville, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Agnes of York, aveva 42 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan De Normanvilleが1225年にイギリスイギリスルトランドエンパンガムで生まれた時，父John de Normanvilleは26歳，母Agnes of Yorkは42歳であった。彼女はSir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnamとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女はイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan De Normanville(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드 핀그함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Normanville(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes of York(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas com Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan De Normanville родилась в 1225 г. в местности эмпингхам, рутланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Normanville было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes of York, 42. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. Она умерла в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan De Normanville nació en 1225, en Empingham, Rutland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Normanville, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Agnes of York, tenía 42 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
